              EXECUTION NOTES (per memory-governance §2.5b — Opus-direct escalation):
              Sonnet 4.6 subagent was dispatched with the standard tightly-scoped Tier-2 task prompt (~25K estimated tokens, well under the 30K budget). Sonnet completed step 1 (RED tests) then thrashed at step 2. Runtime emitted "Autocompact is thrashing: the context refilled to the limit within 3 turns of the previous compact, 3 times in a row." Root cause: hook-injection overhead. Every Read call from inside the subagent triggered (a) vercel-plugin auto-suggesters pattern-matching `prisma/schema.prisma` and `app/**` paths and injecting ~1.5K of "use this skill" boilerplate per call (next-forge, vercel-storage, bootstrap, nextjs, next-cache-components — none applicable to this self-hosted Docker project), plus (b) claude-mem prior-observation pointers ~500 tokens per Read. 10 Read calls = ~20K of pure hook overhead — the subagent burned its budget on injected context, not on planned reads. Per §4 thrashing rule: STOPPED the agent and did NOT re-dispatch (would thrash the same way). Per §2.5b escalation: completed remaining 5 files Opus-direct (100K budget, can absorb the overhead). Logged 🟡 fix in lessons.md flagging this pattern for future feature work — until hooks gain relevance filtering or vercel-plugin disables auto-suggest for non-Vercel projects, expect to escalate Tier-2 Phase-7 work to Opus-direct when hook overhead pushes Sonnet past 30K.

              Future patrol-aware alert wiring: `packages/jobs/src/processors/alerts.processor.ts:111` was intentionally NOT touched this session. Today it creates notifications with `eventId` only — `patrolId` defaults to null. A future task can either (a) derive patrolId from an Event.patrolId column (requires Event schema change + EarthRanger sync update) or (b) add patrol-specific alert rules where rule.scope = "patrol" implies patrolId on the resulting notification. Either path is independent of this FK migration — the scaffolding is now in place to support both.
